source_paper_title: On the Uniform Convergence of Subdifferentials in Stochastic Optimization and Learning
context:
Section 7 (Discussion), page 24 (PDF page 24/42 in the provided parsed text)

There are many interesting future directions to extend the scope of the current work. Notable examples include:
• Can Theorem 5 be extended to other weakly-convex models, such as max-of-smooth functions [56, 57]? Extending this theorem could provide tools for solving other weakly-convex optimization problems.
title: Extend uniform subdifferential convergence rates (Theorem 5) to max-of-smooth weakly convex objectives
background_latex:
Let \(d\in\mathbb{N}\). Let \(\Xi\) be a measurable space with probability measure \(P\), and let \(\xi_1,\dots,\xi_m\) be i.i.d. samples from \(P\) with empirical measure \(P_m\). Let \(O\subseteq\mathbb{R}^d\) be an open set, \(X\subseteq\mathbb{R}^d\) be nonempty, closed, and convex, and let \(R:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}\) be closed and convex. For a (locally) weakly convex function \(\psi\), write \(\partial \psi(x)\) for its Fr\'echet subdifferential at \(x\), and for two nonempty compact sets \(A,B\subseteq\mathbb{R}^d\) write \(H(A,B)\) for their Hausdorff distance. Consider a stochastic objective \(f:\mathbb{R}^d\times\Xi\to\mathbb{R}\) and define the population and empirical objectives
\[\varphi(x)=\mathbb{E}_{\xi\sim P}[f(x,\xi)] + R(x) + \iota_X(x),\qquad \varphi_S(x)=\mathbb{E}_{\xi\sim P_m}[f(x,\xi)] + R(x) + \iota_X(x).\]
In the paper, Theorem 5 gives high-probability bounds on \(\sup_{x\in X\cap B(x_0;r)} H(\partial \varphi(x),\partial \varphi_S(x))\) for a convex-composite class \(f(x,\xi)=h(c(x;\xi))\) with one-dimensional convex \(h\) and smooth \(c(\cdot;\xi)\), under tail and VC-dimension assumptions.
problem_definition_latex: Extend Theorem 5 (a high-probability \(m^{-1/2}\)-type uniform convergence rate for \(\sup_{x\in X\cap B(x_0;r)} H(\partial \varphi(x),\partial \varphi_S(x))\)) from the stochastic convex-composite model \(f(x,\xi)=h(c(x;\xi))\) to other weakly convex stochastic models, specifically to objectives where \(f(x,\xi)\) is a max-of-smooth function in \(x\) (for each \(\xi\)), i.e., establish analogous assumptions and a corresponding explicit rate bound for \(\sup_{x\in X\cap B(x_0;r)} H(\partial \varphi(x),\partial \varphi_S(x))\) when \(f(\cdot,\xi)\) has the max-of-smooth structure.
latex:
Let \(d\in\mathbb{N}\). Let \(\Xi\) be a measurable space with probability measure \(P\), and let \(\xi_1,\dots,\xi_m\) be i.i.d. samples from \(P\) with empirical measure \(P_m\). Let \(O\subseteq\mathbb{R}^d\) be an open set, \(X\subseteq\mathbb{R}^d\) be nonempty, closed, and convex, and let \(R:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}\) be closed and convex. For a (locally) weakly convex function \(\psi\), write \(\partial \psi(x)\) for its Fr\'echet subdifferential at \(x\), and for two nonempty compact sets \(A,B\subseteq\mathbb{R}^d\) write \(H(A,B)\) for their Hausdorff distance. Consider a stochastic objective \(f:\mathbb{R}^d\times\Xi\to\mathbb{R}\) and define the population and empirical objectives
\[\varphi(x)=\mathbb{E}_{\xi\sim P}[f(x,\xi)] + R(x) + \iota_X(x),\qquad \varphi_S(x)=\mathbb{E}_{\xi\sim P_m}[f(x,\xi)] + R(x) + \iota_X(x).\]
In the paper, Theorem 5 gives high-probability bounds on \(\sup_{x\in X\cap B(x_0;r)} H(\partial \varphi(x),\partial \varphi_S(x))\) for a convex-composite class \(f(x,\xi)=h(c(x;\xi))\) with one-dimensional convex \(h\) and smooth \(c(\cdot;\xi)\), under tail and VC-dimension assumptions.

Extend Theorem 5 (a high-probability \(m^{-1/2}\)-type uniform convergence rate for \(\sup_{x\in X\cap B(x_0;r)} H(\partial \varphi(x),\partial \varphi_S(x))\)) from the stochastic convex-composite model \(f(x,\xi)=h(c(x;\xi))\) to other weakly convex stochastic models, specifically to objectives where \(f(x,\xi)\) is a max-of-smooth function in \(x\) (for each \(\xi\)), i.e., establish analogous assumptions and a corresponding explicit rate bound for \(\sup_{x\in X\cap B(x_0;r)} H(\partial \varphi(x),\partial \varphi_S(x))\) when \(f(\cdot,\xi)\) has the max-of-smooth structure.
